title: quercus acutissima subsp. acutissima
intro: Quercus acutissima subsp. acutissima, also known as sawtooth oak, is a deciduous tree species native to East Asia, characterized by its distinctive saw-toothed leaves. The sawtooth oak is valued for its medicinal properties, including antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of sawtooth oak include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a potential remedy for various health conditions. Sawtooth oak is often used in traditional herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
